messages.content: A truck driver goes the wrong way on a one way street. Two police cars see him but don't stop him. Why is that?

The police may have seen the truck driver go the wrong way on the one-way street but decided not to stop him because they were not in a position to safely do so or because they had other, higher priorities in mind, such as responding to an emergency situation.
Those are reasonable possibilities. But there is a simple answer. Try again.

The police may have seen the truck driver go the wrong way on the one-way street but decided not to stop him because they were not in a position to safely do so or because they had other, higher priorities in mind, such as responding to an emergency situation.
That is not the answer. Check for hidden assumptions.

The police may have seen the truck driver go the wrong way on the one-way street but decided not to stop him because they were not in a position to safely do so or because they had other, higher priorities in mind, such as responding to an emergency situation. Additionally, the police may have assumed that the truck driver was unaware that he was going the wrong way and did not want to cause an accident by suddenly stopping him.
